evals.prompt: Render the research findings in ./findings into a JATS (NISO Z39.96) XML article and save it to ./article.xml. Build it from the findings and their cited primary sources — never from a rendered report. Compose a scholarly article with article metadata, a body of one section per surviving finding, and a back-matter reference list of the primary sources.
evals.expected_output: The skill loads the surviving findings, composes well-formed JATS XML grounded in the findings + their primary-source citations (NOT a rendered report): a root <article> with <front>/<article-meta>, a <body> of one <sec> per finding, and <back>/<ref-list> of the primary sources. No internal-research identity leaks into the body.

deterministic_checks.description: A JATS XML article was written
The output has a root <article> element
The article carries <front>/<article-meta> metadata
The article has a <body>
The article has a <back>/<ref-list> reference list
The reference list cites the findings' primary sources
No internal MIF identity (any urn:mif: string) leaks into the rendered JATS file
No internal f_<dimension>_<n> finding handles leak into the rendered body

expectations.description: The JATS article is built from the findings and their primary sources, not from a rendered report
expectations.criteria: The response/transcript shows the skill reading the findings JSON and composing the JATS XML from them + their citation URLs — it does not read or serialize a pre-rendered report.md

evals.prompt: Render the findings in ./findings to JATS XML at ./article.xml. Every surviving finding must appear as a <sec> in the <body>, each linked to its primary source via an <xref ref-type="bibr"> into a <back>/<ref-list> <ref> entry. Verify the current NISO Z39.96 (JATS) version before tagging and record the chosen dtd-version on the root element.
evals.expected_output: Well-formed JATS XML: a with one per surviving finding, each with a claim and <p> content, an <xref ref-type="bibr"> linking to a <ref> in <back>/<ref-list>, and a dtd-version attribute on <article>. The skill confirms the live JATS version (v1.4, Oct 2024).

deterministic_checks.description: A JATS XML article was written
The root <article> records a JATS dtd-version
The body has a <sec> with a <title> for the finding
Each claim links to its citation via an <xref ref-type="bibr">
The reference list cites the finding's primary source
No internal MIF identity (any urn:mif: string) leaks into the rendered JATS file
No internal f_<dimension>_<n> finding handles leak into the rendered body

expectations.description: The skill verifies the live JATS version before tagging
expectations.criteria: The transcript shows the skill confirming the current NISO Z39.96 / JATS version (v1.4, Oct 2024) rather than trusting a cached version string

evals.prompt: Render the findings in ./findings to JATS XML at ./article.xml. One finding is falsified and must be excluded. The article body must contain only the surviving finding, and the back-matter <ref-list> must list only its primary source.
evals.expected_output: The skill excludes the falsified finding, emitting a JATS article whose <body> contains only the surviving finding's <sec> and whose <back>/<ref-list> cites only its primary source. The falsified finding's claim text and citation do not appear.

deterministic_checks.description: A JATS XML article was written
The body contains the surviving finding section
The reference list cites the surviving finding's primary source
The falsified finding's primary source is excluded
No internal MIF identity (any urn:mif: string) leaks into the rendered JATS file
No internal f_<dimension>_<n> finding handles leak into the rendered body

expectations.description: The skill excludes falsified findings from the rendered article
expectations.criteria: The transcript shows the skill filtering on verification.verdict so the falsified finding is omitted from both the body and the reference list
